results.title: In Fabric
results.overview: A haunting ghost story set against the backdrop of a busy winter sales period in a department store, following the life of a cursed dress as it passes from person to person, with devastating consequences.

results.title: A Tear in the Fabric of Life
results.original_title: A Tear in the Fabric of Life
results.overview: After an accident takes the life of their partner, an individual grieves and mourns, exploring the inability to let go. The animated short film of the EP "A Tear in the Fabric of Life" by Knocked Loose.

results.title: A Twist in the Fabric of Space
results.original_title: A Twist in the Fabric of Space
results.overview: Mankind distinguishes himself from the animal due to the ability of making goals in the future. He conceives of his life as a project. This project can be crossed with his awareness of death.

results.title: Fabricated in the Actual Arctic (After Nanook)
results.original_title: Fabricated in the Actual Arctic (After Nanook)
results.overview: A silent essay considers the legacy of Robert Flaherty’s 1922 documentary, Nanook of the North (also known as, A Story of Life and Love in the Actual Arctic). An image by an Inuk artist is discovered “missing” from the Museum of Modern Art collection.

results.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
results.overview: A young boy wins a tour through the most magnificent chocolate factory in the world, led by the world's most unusual candy maker.

results.title: 5 Factories: Worker Control in Venezuela
results.overview: 5 Factories provides a penetrating look at the Bolivarian socio-economic project designed to challenge the dominant neo-liberal development model. Since the election of Hugo Chávez in 1998, the Venezuelan government has implemented reforms to transform the nation into what Chávez and his supporters refer to as a form of democratic socialism. As a component of this economic transformation, the government has supported co-ownership initiatives in which workers’ councils play a key role in company management. 5 Factories provides a unique perspective on the Bolivarian experiment, examining the successes and challenges of five companies rejecting traditional ideas of industrial management.

results.title: Babes in Toyland
results.overview: With just days to go before presents are scheduled for Christmas delivery, Toyland is buzzing with activity. However, curmudgeonly creditor Barnaby Crookedman is hatching a devious scheme to destroy Toyland – meaning no presents for the world's children! Siblings Jack and Jill, alongside toy factory workers Tom and Mary, rush into action to stop the villain, receiving some assistance from the likes of Humpty Dumpty and even Santa Claus himself.

results.title: A Visit to the Champagne Factory Mercier
results.original_title: Ein Besuch in der Champagnerfabrik Mercier
results.overview: A visit to the Champagne Factory Mercier.

results.title: The Making of Damascus Gun Barrels in Nessonvaux (Belgium)
results.original_title: La fabrication des canons de fusils à Nessonvaux (Belgique)
results.overview: Investigation by the Museum of Walloon Life showing the different stages of the manufacture of rifle barrels using the so-called "Damascus" process, in Nessonvaux in 1925 and 1931

results.title: Doraemon: Nobita and the Spiral City
results.original_title: 映画ドラえもん のび太のねじ巻き都市冒険記
results.overview: When Doraemon and Nobita win a tiny planet through a lottery, they make use of a screw to bring several toys to life and create a toy town.

results.title: Tom and Jerry: Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory
results.original_title: Tom and Jerry: Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory
results.overview: The classic Roald Dahl tale gets a modern twist when Tom and Jerry enter the amazing world of Willy Wonka's Chocolate Factory.

results.title: American Factory
results.overview: In post-industrial Ohio, a Chinese billionaire opens a new factory in the husk of an abandoned General Motors plant, hiring two thousand blue-collar Americans. Early days of hope and optimism give way to setbacks as high-tech China clashes with working-class America.

results.title: Andy Warhol: Made in China
results.overview: A look at the man behind the legend, capturing the real Andy Warhol, as an artist and as a person, as he travels through China, from Hong Kong's glitter to the mystique of Peking's Forbidden City. Set in the Far East, the story begins with the opening of the most elegant jet set watering hole in Asia, Hong Kong's “I Club,” whose owner, a young Chinese millionaire, decided to try an experiment: to transplant the most advanced, far-out Western culture to the Far East in a multimillion-dollar club that offers everything from restaurants and bars, to a health club and even an art gallery. Warhol is invited to attend the opening as a guest of honor showing his “Celebrity Portraits.” The result of this cultural experiment was varied. Emotions from the “I Club” and Warhol's work ranged from outrage to indifference to wonder.

results.title: American Factory: A Conversation with the Obamas
results.original_title: American Factory: A Conversation with the Obamas
results.overview: Barack and Michelle Obama talk with the directors of the documentary American Factory about the importance of storytelling and the impact of their film.
